title: TraceMap Home
summary: Start with the deterministic static evidence boundary, then follow routes to proof, limitations, docs, and demo pages.
limitations: Home-page copy is orientation, not the source of proof.
Claims must route to public evidence pages or repository documents.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, deployment state, endpoint performance, or release approval proof.
No AI impact analysis, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Capabilities
summary: Demo-level capability map for scanner, reducer, report, language adapter, and review-packet surfaces with status labels.
limitations: Capability rows are status-framed and should not be upgraded beyond their supporting public proof.
Future and dev-only rows remain bounded until main evidence exists.
nonClaims: No release approval, operational safety, endpoint performance, or deployment state proof.
No raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, or logs/analyzer.log are published.

title: Docs
summary: Public documentation hub linking source-of-truth repository docs, validation notes, acceptance criteria, and packet guidance.
limitations: The docs hub is navigation; repository docs remain the source-of-truth references.
Readers should inspect validation and limitations before summarizing product behavior.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, release approval, or absence-of-impact proof.
No AI impact analysis or LLM-based product feature claim.

title: Evidence Model
summary: Explains rule IDs, evidence tiers, file spans, coverage labels, and why public conclusions need supporting proof.
limitations: Static evidence can show source relationships and gaps, not operational behavior.
Tier labels describe evidence strength and analysis gaps.
nonClaims: No production traffic, branch feasibility, deployment state, endpoint performance, or release approval proof.
No prompt-based classification or AI impact analysis in the scanner or reducer.

title: Output Artifacts
summary: Names the scanner outputs and describes which artifact families stay public-safe versus local-only.
limitations: Artifact names do not replace generated evidence rows, rule IDs, tiers, spans, or coverage labels.
Private repository artifacts stay local unless explicitly redacted and reviewed.
nonClaims: No raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, or logs/analyzer.log are public discovery outputs.
No production usage, deployment state, release approval, or runtime behavior proof.

title: Validation
summary: Lists deterministic checks, smoke runs, sample-repo validation, generated-output guards, and artifact acceptance criteria.
limitations: Validation commands show the checked scope of a change, not broad operational certainty.
Language-adapter coverage depends on the pinned checks and sample repositories named by the docs.
nonClaims: No release approval, production traffic proof, endpoint performance proof, or clean bill of health under reduced coverage.
No embeddings, vector databases, LLM calls, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Evidence Packet Guide
summary: Shows how public-safe packets keep summaries attached to proof paths, rule IDs, evidence tiers, coverage labels, and limitations.
limitations: Packet copy must stay attached to supporting proof and coverage labels.
Visual or narrative packet layers do not replace generated artifacts.
nonClaims: No production usage, runtime reachability, release safety, or release approval proof.
No raw fact streams, SQLite indexes, analyzer logs, raw SQL, secrets, or local output roots are public packet content.

title: Limitations
summary: Starts with what TraceMap cannot prove so readers keep static evidence separate from operational claims.
limitations: Reduced coverage must remain labeled and cannot support absence-of-impact statements.
Static analysis cannot prove operational state or release readiness.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, deployment state, endpoint performance, release approval, or release safety proof.
No AI impact analysis, LLM calls, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Public Demo Result
summary: Current demo result guide with generated summary rows, rule IDs, evidence tiers, coverage labels, counts, and reasons.
limitations: Demo counts and statuses are tied to checked-in samples and local toolchain coverage.
Raw local artifacts are not public discovery content.
nonClaims: No full-impact proof, runtime behavior proof, production traffic proof, production dependency understanding, endpoint performance proof, or release safety proof.
No facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, logs/analyzer.log, raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, or secrets are published.

title: Demo Walkthrough
summary: Guided public demo route for running checked-in samples, reading summaries, following static evidence, and reviewing limitations.
limitations: The walkthrough is scoped to checked-in samples and local generated outputs.
Deeper artifacts stay local unless a public-safe summary exists.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production traffic, release safety, release approval, or AI impact analysis proof.
No raw source snippets, raw SQL, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, logs/analyzer.log, or local output roots are published.

title: Demo Proof Upgrades
summary: Evidence ledger for demo rows that moved from deferred to reproducible public-demo proof with coverage labels.
limitations: Proof rows are public-demo evidence from samples, not general production proof.
Rows retain partial-analysis and gap labels where coverage is reduced.
nonClaims: No runtime topology, production usage, production dependency understanding, endpoint performance, release approval, or release safety proof.
No raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, combined SQLite files, or analyzer logs are published.

title: Demo Proof Assets
summary: Public-safe visual orientation for generated demo summaries and report families without replacing source-backed proof.
limitations: Visual assets are secondary orientation over rule-backed evidence and limitations.
Generated summaries and reports remain the proof paths.
nonClaims: No full-impact proof, runtime reachability proof, production dependency understanding, endpoint performance, release approval, or release safety proof.
No raw fact streams, SQLite indexes, analyzer logs, local output roots, raw SQL, or secrets are published.

title: Proof Path Index
summary: Public-demo index that maps TraceMap public routes and demo sections to artifact type, rule ID or rule family, evidence tier, coverage label, proof path, limitation, and public status.
limitations: The index is a route map over public-safe proof surfaces; it does not replace generated artifacts, rule documentation, or repository docs.
Raw scan artifacts and SQLite files remain local-only unless a future public-safe sample explicitly checks in sanitized output.
nonClaims: No runtime proof, production traffic proof, endpoint performance proof, deployment state proof, release safety proof, or release approval proof.

No AI impact analysis, LLM calls, embeddings, vector databases, prompt-based classification, raw facts, SQLite indexes, analyzer logs, raw SQL, secrets, local paths, raw remotes, or generated scan directories are published.

title: Roadmap Gates
summary: Explains how public copy moves from concept to demo only when public-safe evidence and limitations exist.
limitations: Roadmap entries are planning notes, not proof.
Concept rows need checked-in samples, generated summaries, and visible limitations before promotion.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production traffic, deployment state, endpoint performance, release safety, or AI impact analysis proof.
No LLM calls, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Incident Review Orientation
summary: Describes how static evidence can orient incident-adjacent code questions while operational proof stays outside TraceMap.
limitations: This is a bounded workflow idea, not proof of production cause or operational state.
Telemetry, logs, traces, tests, and humans remain responsible for runtime proof.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production traffic, deployment state, endpoint performance, P1 root cause, release safety, or release approval proof.
No AI impact analysis or prompt-based product classification.

title: Repository README
summary: Source-of-truth project overview for CLI goals, required scan outputs, demo commands, and product boundaries.
limitations: The README describes repository behavior and command intent; run validation for current local results.
Public site pages may summarize the README but should not strengthen it.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, deployment state, endpoint performance, or release approval proof.
No LLM calls, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in TraceMap core.

title: Acceptance Criteria
summary: Source-of-truth criteria for artifacts, reducer behavior, evidence rows, coverage labels, and public-safe packet expectations.
limitations: Acceptance criteria define expected behavior but still require implementation and validation evidence.
Criteria should be read with the rule catalog and validation docs.
nonClaims: No production traffic proof, runtime reachability proof, deployment state proof, endpoint performance proof, or release approval proof.
No raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, or logs/analyzer.log are published by discovery metadata.

title: Validation Guide
summary: Source-of-truth validation guide for deterministic tests, smoke checks, sample repositories, and language-adapter checks.
limitations: Validation is scoped to the commands, samples, and adapters named by the guide.
Deferred checks must be recorded plainly when not run.
nonClaims: No release approval, production usage, endpoint performance, deployment state, or absence-of-impact proof under reduced coverage.
No AI impact analysis, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Language Adapter Contract
summary: Source-of-truth adapter contract for facts, relationships, reports, coverage labels, and reducer-compatible evidence.
limitations: Adapter maturity depends on each language implementation and its pinned checks.
Syntax fallback remains useful but must be labeled when semantic coverage is reduced.
nonClaims: No runtime traffic, production usage, deployment state, endpoint performance, release safety, or release approval proof.
No LLM calls, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in adapter extraction.

title: Rule Catalog
summary: Source-of-truth rule catalog used to connect evidence rows to documented rule IDs and limitations.
limitations: Rule documentation bounds what each rule can and cannot support.
A rule ID is required before public discovery metadata can summarize an evidence conclusion.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, release approval, or endpoint performance proof.
No AI impact analysis or prompt-based classification rule.
